es.title: Rotata | The system behind B2B growth
es.description: Rotata designs B2B operating systems that connect CRM, data, automation, conversion and reporting.
es.h1: We build the system behind B2B growth
es.intro: Rotata turns fragmented revenue operations into one operating model that marketing and sales can read, run and improve.

sections.heading: Market problem: fragmentation
sections.body: Growth breaks when CRM, targeting, follow-up and reporting run as separate projects.
items: Different teams use different stage logic
Lead quality is debated instead of defined
Signals do not reach the owner who should act
Reporting describes activity but not movement

sections.heading: System overview
sections.body: The system connects inputs, rules, workflows and outcomes in one commercial sequence.

sections.heading: Solution pillars
sections.body: Each pillar fixes a different source of commercial friction.

items.title: Pipeline
items.text: Make stage movement, ownership and bottlenecks readable.

items.title: Intelligence
items.text: Use fit, enrichment and signals to set priority.

items.title: Conversion
items.text: Reduce avoidable drop-off between touchpoints and teams.

items.title: Alignment
items.text: Keep marketing and sales inside one operating language.

sections.heading: Services snapshot
sections.body: Rotata delivers connected capabilities inside the wider system.

sections.heading: Process snapshot
sections.body: The order matters because bad structure should not be automated faster.

sections.heading: Proof
sections.body: Cases and operating metrics matter only when the system becomes easier to read and govern in live use.

sections.heading: Ecosystem
sections.body: Platforms support the architecture. The system remains the product.

sections.heading: ROI framing
sections.body: Return improves when the system removes expensive friction.

es.title: About Rotata | Operating clarity for B2B teams
es.description: Rotata is a B2B systems company focused on CRM, data, workflows, conversion and commercial alignment.
es.h1: Rotata is built around operating clarity
es.intro: Rotata exists to turn disconnected revenue work into one commercial system people can trust.

sections.heading: Who we are
sections.body: Rotata is a systems company for B2B teams that need revenue operations to become legible, repeatable and commercially useful.

sections.heading: What we do
sections.body: Rotata designs the structure that lets tools and teams operate coherently.

items.title: Commercial architecture
items.text: Define lifecycle, pipeline, ownership and stage logic before automation.

items.title: CRM and data structure
items.text: Align objects, fields and routing rules to real decisions.

items.title: Workflows and handoffs
items.text: Make revenue work move through one operating rhythm.

items.title: Reporting discipline
items.text: Make dashboards read the same model the team uses every day.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.body: Most revenue friction comes from systems added in parts and never made to behave as one.
items: Volume rises while qualification stays weak
Automation runs without a reliable rule
Teams debate definitions instead of reading one model
The tool stack grows while visibility gets worse

sections.heading: How we think
sections.body: System design comes first because every later decision depends on it.
items: Start from the real sales motion

sections.heading: Differentiation
sections.body: The difference is how the work is framed and controlled.

items.title: System before tool
items.text: Platforms are chosen for fit inside the architecture.

items.title: Cross-functional by default
items.text: Marketing, sales, CRM, data and reporting are designed together.

items.title: Built for operators
items.text: The output has to work for the people using it every day.

items.title: Proof over promises
items.text: Claims stay tied to structure and visible commercial movement.

sections.heading: Trust signals
sections.body: Trust comes from disciplined operating work.

es.description: Rotata structures B2B growth through connected pipeline, intelligence, conversion and alignment systems.
es.h1: The solution is a connected revenue system
es.intro: Rotata fixes revenue friction by redesigning how data, qualification, workflows and reporting work together.

sections.heading: Market breakdown
sections.body: Most teams do not need more activity first. They need a system that stops activity from pulling in different directions.

items.title: Pipeline
items.text: Make stage logic, ownership and movement readable.

items.text: Use fit, enrichment and signals to focus attention.

items.title: Conversion
items.text: Reduce avoidable loss between touchpoints and teams.

items.title: Alignment
items.text: Keep marketing and sales inside one commercial readout.

sections.heading: System explanation
sections.body: Tools, teams and dashboards should all read the same operating structure.

sections.heading: Before vs after
sections.body: The goal is not cosmetic improvement. It is a different operating experience.
items: Before: interpretive stages / After: explicit criteria
Before: manual routing / After: rule-based ownership
Before: signals outside CRM / After: signals inside workflow
Before: dashboards need explanation / After: dashboards read one model

sections.heading: Outcomes
sections.body: A better system makes commercial conversations more precise.

sections.heading: Who it is for / not for
sections.body: The fit is strongest when the company wants system change rather than isolated delivery.
items: For: B2B teams already using a live CRM
For: companies with reporting that still lacks trust
Not for: tool shopping without ownership

es.title: HubSpot, structured for how your business actually works | Rotata
es.description: HubSpot should not be a repository of activity. It should be the infrastructure that makes selling, measuring and deciding easier.
es.h1: HubSpot, structured for how your business actually works
es.intro: HubSpot should not be a repository of activity. It should be the infrastructure that makes selling, measuring and deciding easier.

sections.heading: Context
sections.body: HubSpot becomes powerful when the business structure underneath it is clear. Without that structure, the platform simply exposes confusion faster.

sections.heading: Problem
sections.body: Teams often buy HubSpot expecting clarity, but import messy data, unclear stages, duplicated fields and workflows that mirror old habits.

sections.heading: What Rotata does
sections.body: We structure HubSpot around the real commercial process: data model, lifecycle stages, pipelines, workflows, lists, dashboards and handoffs.

items.title: Diagnose
items.text: Map the current setup and identify structural blockers.

items.title: Design
items.text: Define the operating model, data rules and conversion path.

items.title: Implement
items.text: Configure the system in tools the team already uses.

items.title: Measure
items.text: Connect dashboards and feedback loops to business decisions.

sections.heading: Outcomes
sections.body: HubSpot should make decisions easier: who to prioritize, what changed, what sales needs, and where pipeline is blocked.

sections.heading: When it is relevant
sections.body: This matters when HubSpot exists but reporting is weak, automation is inconsistent, sales adoption is low or leadership cannot trust the data.

sections.heading: System integration
sections.body: HubSpot connects to the wider growth system: acquisition, outbound, account intelligence, analytics and reporting rhythm.

es.title: Better data, used properly | Rotata
es.description: More data does not equal more growth. Value appears when account intelligence connects to CRM and sales process.
es.h1: Better data, used properly
es.intro: More data does not equal more growth. Value appears when account intelligence connects to CRM and sales process.

sections.heading: Context
sections.body: Better data only creates value when it is structured, filtered and connected to the commercial workflow.

sections.heading: Problem
sections.body: More contacts can create more noise. Without targeting logic, data quality rules and CRM routing, teams burn time on the wrong accounts.

sections.heading: What Rotata does
sections.body: We turn ZoomInfo into a usable data layer: ICP definition, segmentation, enrichment logic, routing, lists and CRM activation.

es.title: Outbound that is structured, not improvised | Rotata
es.description: We design multichannel prospecting with targeting, message relevance, sequences and performance tracking.
es.h1: Outbound that is structured, not improvised
es.intro: We design multichannel prospecting with targeting, message relevance, sequences and performance tracking.

sections.heading: Context
sections.body: Outbound works when targeting, timing, message relevance and measurement are structured as one system.

sections.heading: Problem
sections.body: Improvised outbound creates low response rates, inconsistent follow-up and no clear learning loop between sales activity and CRM data.

sections.heading: What Rotata does
sections.body: We design the outbound system: ICP, account lists, data checks, multichannel sequences, messaging, CRM sync and performance dashboards.

es.title: ROI | Estimate the economic effect of a better system
es.description: Use Rotata’s ROI framing to estimate how a better revenue system can improve conversion, speed and commercial visibility.
es.h1: Use ROI to judge structure before buying more activity
es.intro: The calculator is a framing tool. It helps a team estimate the value of fixing operating friction before it commits to new spend.

sections.heading: Why ROI is misunderstood
sections.body: ROI is often framed as software output instead of system behaviour.
items: Tool cost is isolated from process quality
Lead volume is confused with commercial value
Reporting is too weak to support the estimate

sections.heading: Inputs
sections.body: A useful estimate starts with a small number of commercial inputs.

sections.heading: Calculation logic
sections.body: The logic should stay simple enough to explain in one conversation.
items: Use the result as a planning tool

sections.heading: Results
sections.body: The result should be read as economic room created by better operating design.

sections.heading: Interpretation
sections.body: The ROI estimate is directional. It is useful because it makes assumptions visible before a deeper diagnostic.

sections.heading: Scenarios
sections.body: Different system changes create different ROI patterns.

es.final_cta: Turn the estimate into a real system review

sections.body: The route fits teams that need a structural conversation, not a generic sales exchange.

sections.heading: Trust signals
sections.body: The route is designed to reduce unnecessary noise at the decision point.

es.description: Anonymized case patterns showing how Rotata changes CRM structure, workflow control and commercial visibility.
es.h1: Cases explained through the system applied
es.intro: The cases are written as operating patterns. The point is to show how the change happens, not to inflate the result.

sections.heading: Case overview
sections.body: Each case starts with the operating problem, shows the system layer applied and ends with the change that became visible.

sections.heading: Problem snapshots
sections.body: The recurring case themes are structural, not cosmetic.
items: CRM stages do not match the buying motion
Signals are visible but not actionable

sections.heading: System applied
sections.body: The work usually combines several layers because one isolated fix rarely holds.

items.title: Redefine the model
items.text: Rebuild objects, stages and required inputs around one commercial reality.

items.title: Connect workflows
items.text: Move routing, tasking and ownership through explicit rules.

items.title: Sync data and signals
items.text: Feed enrichment and engagement into priority and action.

items.title: Rebuild reporting
items.text: Make dashboards read the same structure used by the team.

sections.heading: Results highlights
sections.body: The result is that the commercial system becomes usable in live operating reviews.

sections.heading: Key insights
sections.body: The same lessons show up repeatedly across implementations.

es.title: Rotata Process | Diagnose, design, implement, optimize
es.description: The structured process Rotata uses to turn fragmented revenue operations into one operating system.
es.h1: A process built to make system change stick
es.intro: Rotata uses a fixed sequence so the operating break is understood before the build starts.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: The work moves from diagnosis to operating rhythm. Each stage exists to make the next one more accurate.

sections.heading: Diagnose
sections.body: Diagnosis is used to find the real operating break, not just the loudest symptom.

sections.heading: Design
sections.body: The target state is defined before tools are changed.

sections.heading: Implement
sections.body: Implementation turns the model into live infrastructure.

items.title: Configure the CRM core
items.text: Align objects, fields, stages and permissions to the design.

items.title: Connect data and signals
items.text: Wire enrichment, triggers and ownership logic into the flow.

items.title: Launch workflows
items.text: Move rules into automation only after review and documentation.

items.title: Train operators
items.text: Teach the logic behind the system, not just the clicks.

sections.heading: Optimize
sections.body: Optimization uses observed movement to improve the model without breaking it.

sections.heading: Feedback loops
sections.body: The system stays healthy when review is built in.
items: Retrain owners when the process shifts

es.final_cta: Review the process against the current setup

es.title: Privacy policy | How Rotata handles personal data
es.description: Read how Rotata collects, uses, stores and protects personal data through rotata.es and related contact flows.
es.h1: Privacy policy
es.intro: This page explains how Rotata handles personal data collected through the site, contact forms and consent-aware analytics setup.

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.body: This policy covers personal data collected through rotata.es and related direct contact channels.

sections.heading: Data controller details
sections.body: Rotata is responsible for the processing described on this page.

sections.heading: Data collected
sections.body: Rotata keeps collection limited to what is relevant for the contact and operating context.
items: Analytics data only where consent allows it

sections.heading: How data is collected
sections.body: Data enters through direct user action and consent-aware technical flows.
items: Configured analytics or marketing scripts when consent allows it
Local browser storage for consent choices

sections.heading: Purpose of processing
sections.body: Each processing purpose should tie back to a clear business reason.

items.title: Respond to inquiries
items.text: Read the issue and answer with next steps.

items.title: Assess fit
items.text: Understand the operating context before proposing a relevant conversation.

items.title: Improve the site
items.text: Use consented analytics to understand usage and improvement needs.

items.title: Protect the site
items.text: Use basic anti-spam and operational controls.

sections.heading: Legal basis
sections.body: The legal basis depends on the context of the processing.
items: Legitimate interest in site security and continuity

sections.heading: Data sharing
sections.body: Rotata limits sharing to processors or providers needed to run the service.
items: Configured analytics or marketing providers when consent exists
Professional service providers under confidentiality duties

sections.heading: Data retention
sections.body: Retention follows necessity, contractual context and legal obligations.
items: Inquiry data kept as long as needed for the conversation
Analytics retention depends on provider settings
Consent choices remain until changed or cleared
Records may be retained longer where law requires it

sections.heading: User rights
sections.body: Users may exercise applicable data protection rights.

sections.heading: Contact / data requests
sections.body: Data requests should be sent with enough context for Rotata to respond responsibly.
items: Reasonable identity verification may be required
Users may contact the competent supervisory authority if needed

es.final_cta: Contact Rotata about a data request

es.title: Cookie policy | Browser storage and consent on rotata.es
es.description: Learn how rotata.es uses necessary cookies, local storage and consent-controlled third-party scripts.
es.h1: Cookie policy
es.intro: This page explains how the site uses browser storage, necessary cookies and consent-controlled third-party scripts.

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.body: rotata.es uses a mix of necessary browser storage and optional third-party scripts that only activate after consent where applicable.

sections.heading: What cookies are
sections.body: Cookies are small browser files. The site also uses local browser storage to remember consent choices and keep the experience consistent.

sections.heading: Types of cookies used
sections.body: The consent model is organized into clear categories.

sections.heading: Purpose of cookies
sections.body: Storage and scripts are used only for specific operational reasons.
items: Measure usage where analytics consent exists
Load marketing providers only when consent exists

sections.heading: Third-party cookies
sections.body: Third-party tools are only relevant when they are configured and consent allows them.
items: Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics

sections.heading: Cookie duration
sections.body: Duration depends on the storage type and provider involved.
items: Consent choices remain until changed or cleared
Third-party providers apply their own durations
Necessary storage is kept only as long as needed
Users should review provider policies for specifics

sections.heading: Consent management
sections.body: The site gives users direct control over non-essential categories.
items: A consent banner appears until a choice is stored
Accept all enables analytics, marketing and preferences
Necessary only keeps essential storage and behaviour
Consent can be reset by clearing browser storage

sections.heading: How to disable cookies
sections.body: Users can disable or limit storage through several routes.
items: Reject non-essential categories in the banner
Block or clear cookies in browser settings
Clear local storage for a fresh consent choice
Use stricter browser controls where appropriate

sections.heading: Updates to policy
sections.body: This policy changes when the storage logic, configured providers or consent model changes.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body: Questions about cookies or browser storage can be sent through Rotata’s contact routes.

es.final_cta: Use contact for a cookie question

es.title: Accessibility | Usability commitments for rotata.es
es.description: Read Rotata’s accessibility commitments, current scope, known limitations and feedback process for rotata.es.
es.h1: Accessibility statement
es.intro: Rotata aims to keep the site readable, navigable and predictable across modern devices, browsers and input methods.

sections.heading: Commitment statement
sections.body: Rotata works to make rotata.es usable by keeping structure, contrast and interaction clear.

sections.heading: Accessibility standards
sections.body: The site aims to follow WCAG 2.1 AA-aligned practices where practical for its static-first architecture.
items: Visible focus states and readable contrast
Responsive layouts supporting zoom and reflow

sections.heading: Scope of compliance
sections.body: Accessibility work currently focuses on the core published experience.

sections.heading: Measures implemented
sections.body: Accessibility depends on repeatable implementation choices.

items.title: Structured headings
items.text: Pages follow a clear heading hierarchy and semantic landmarks.

items.title: Labeled forms
items.text: Form controls use visible labels and validation messaging.

items.title: Keyboard access
items.text: Navigation and main interactions are reachable without a mouse.

items.title: Readable mobile layout
items.text: Text, spacing and tap targets are designed for small screens first.

sections.heading: Known limitations
sections.body: Some areas still need ongoing review and refinement.
items: Third-party scripts may introduce behaviour outside Rotata’s direct control
Decorative visuals are not relied on for essential meaning
Content updates require periodic accessibility QA
Future integrations should be reviewed before being treated as fully accessible

sections.heading: Assistive technologies supported
sections.body: The site is designed to work with common browser and device accessibility features.
items: Reduced-motion and standard mobile accessibility settings

sections.heading: Feedback mechanism
sections.body: Users can report accessibility issues, unclear interactions or readability problems so Rotata can review them directly.

sections.heading: Improvement roadmap
sections.body: Accessibility issues should move through a visible correction loop.
items: Fix the source component or content pattern

sections.heading: Review / audit process
sections.body: Accessibility quality improves when review is repeated at component and page level.
items: Form review for labels and feedback states

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body: Use the published routes to report accessibility issues or request clarification.

es.final_cta: Use contact to report an accessibility issue

en.title: Rotata Values | Principles that hold the system
en.description: The principles, standards and quality expectations that shape how Rotata designs B2B operating systems.
en.h1: Values that make the system credible
en.intro: Rotata works from operating principles that keep systems clear, stable and commercially useful after launch.

sections.heading: Core principles
sections.body: The principles are practical. They govern how work is scoped, built and reviewed.

items.title: Clarity over volume
items.text: A smaller system that is readable is worth more than a noisy one.

items.title: Structure before automation
items.text: Automation only starts after definitions and ownership are trusted.

items.title: Measurement before opinion
items.text: Claims need a traceable source in the operating model.

items.title: Accountability over motion
items.text: Each rule and workflow needs an owner and a business reason.

sections.heading: Operating standards
sections.body: Standards keep the system usable as teams scale or change.
items: One definition across CRM and dashboards

sections.heading: Decision making
sections.body: Rotata prefers a simple path that can be defended later.

sections.heading: Client philosophy
sections.body: Good work needs a client willing to operate the system seriously.

sections.heading: Team mindset
sections.body: The mindset behind the work should show up in the build itself.

items.title: Systems bias
items.text: Read problems as connected operating issues.

items.title: Commercial realism
items.text: Fit the model to how buying and selling really happen.

items.title: Operator empathy
items.text: Help the people living inside the system.

items.title: Disciplined iteration
items.text: Review, measure and refine rather than restart endlessly.

sections.heading: Quality expectations
sections.body: Quality is visible in what the system prevents as much as what it enables.

en.final_cta: Review values against the current setup

en.title: Pipeline Solution | Make movement readable
en.description: Rotata structures pipeline logic so stages, ownership and reporting reflect real commercial movement.
en.h1: Pipeline becomes readable when the structure is explicit
en.intro: A pipeline is only useful when stage meaning, ownership and reporting are governed by the same operating rule.

sections.heading: Pipeline problem
sections.body: Pipeline control fails when stage criteria, ownership and forecast logic drift apart.
items: Entry and exit criteria are weak
Forecast views drift from CRM reality

sections.heading: Current state
sections.body: The broken state usually shows up as stage noise, weak entry criteria and reporting that needs manual correction.
items: Lifecycle does not match the sales motion
Reporting depends on reconciliation outside the CRM

sections.heading: System approach
sections.body: Rotata rebuilds pipeline structure so movement, ownership and reporting all read from the same commercial rule set.

items.title: Define commercial stages
items.text: Reflect real buying progression rather than software defaults.

items.title: Set required properties
items.text: Ask for data only where a business decision needs it.

items.title: Route by ownership rules
items.text: Move work to the right queue through explicit logic.

items.title: Report from the same model
items.text: Rebuild dashboards on top of the operating definition.

sections.heading: Flow structure
sections.body: The flow should make movement, approval and bottlenecks visible inside live pipeline reviews.

sections.heading: Improvements
sections.body: The first gains show up as cleaner movement, fewer exceptions and more credible forecast conversations.

sections.heading: Outcomes
sections.body: A stronger pipeline layer gives revenue teams one operating language for planning, execution and review.

en.title: Intelligence Solution | Turn signals into priority
en.description: Rotata connects data, enrichment and account signals to commercial prioritization.
en.h1: Intelligence works when signals change the workflow
en.intro: Account intelligence is useful when data, timing and fit alter who gets attention and what the next action should be.

sections.heading: Data problem
sections.body: Account intelligence fails when fit, recency and buying-role coverage are missing at the moment of action.
items: Contacts are incomplete at the moment of action
Reps research what the system should already know

sections.heading: Signal gaps
sections.body: The gap is rarely a lack of data. It is weak trust, missing context and signals that never change workflow priority.
items: Fit data exists but is not trusted
Buying roles are missing or stale
Signals do not create tasks or owner changes

sections.heading: Intelligence layer
sections.body: Rotata builds an intelligence layer that turns data quality and timing into usable commercial priority.

items.title: Enrich accounts
items.text: Fill the gaps that block segmentation and coverage.

items.title: Score fit
items.text: Separate high-fit accounts from background noise.

items.title: Detect signals
items.text: Make intent and engagement part of the account readout.

items.title: Push priority into workflow
items.text: Let the signal change routing, tasking or timing.

sections.heading: Data flow
sections.body: The flow should move cleanly from source data to CRM action without leaving research work to the team.
items: Source and enrich the account data

sections.heading: Insights generation
sections.body: Useful intelligence produces clear readouts the team can act on immediately.

sections.heading: Outcomes
sections.body: The result is a workflow that knows more before the team spends time, budget or attention.
items: Better timing on outreach and follow-up

en.title: Conversion Solution | Remove avoidable drop-off
en.description: Rotata improves conversion by redesigning follow-up, routing, qualification and touchpoint logic.
en.h1: Conversion improves when follow-up is designed
en.intro: Conversion is the result of better qualification, faster routing and a clearer next-step system.

sections.heading: Conversion problem
sections.body: Conversion drops when qualification, routing and follow-up rules break between touchpoints.
items: Inbound sits too long before a next step exists
Replies arrive without a shared routing rule
Follow-up drops because ownership is unclear
The leak is discovered too late

sections.heading: Funnel gaps
sections.body: The leak usually sits between source, owner, message and next-step logic rather than at one isolated channel.
items: Slow response to intent or inbound action
Weak entry criteria into owner queues
Sequences do not match account context
No clear rule for meeting or opportunity quality

sections.heading: System changes
sections.body: Rotata changes the operating rules behind qualification, routing and follow-up so conversion can be reviewed, not guessed.

items.title: Clarify qualification
items.text: Make the handoff rule explicit before volume enters the queue.

items.title: Design response paths
items.text: Different sources should trigger different next actions.

items.title: Connect message to context
items.text: Fit, role and trigger data should shape follow-up.

items.title: Measure stage movement
items.text: Review conversion where the friction actually occurs.

sections.heading: Touchpoints
sections.body: Each touchpoint should carry context forward instead of forcing the next owner to reconstruct it.
items: Intent or inbound action enters the queue
Owner response creates the next step
Meeting or opportunity handoff keeps the context intact

sections.heading: Optimization logic
sections.body: Optimization should happen where the leak is visible, not where activity happens to be easiest to measure.

sections.heading: Outcomes
sections.body: A better conversion layer creates cleaner funnel movement and clearer accountability for what happens next.

en.title: Alignment Solution | One commercial read across teams
en.description: Rotata aligns marketing and sales through shared definitions, routing rules, workflows and reporting.
en.h1: Alignment happens when teams read the same system
en.intro: Marketing and sales align when the system gives them one stage language, one handoff model and one feedback loop.

sections.heading: Misalignment problem
sections.body: Alignment breaks when marketing and sales use different definitions, owners and success measures.
items: MQL and SQL mean different things in different rooms
Sales feedback does not return in a usable format

sections.heading: Sales vs marketing gap
sections.body: The gap shows up in duplicated data, disputed lead quality and reporting that hides disagreement instead of resolving it.
items: Different success measures drive different behaviour
Rejection reasons are not reviewed cleanly

sections.heading: System alignment
sections.body: Rotata aligns teams by giving both sides one qualification language, one routing model and one reporting reality.

items.title: Shared definitions
items.text: Qualification and stage logic mean the same thing everywhere.

items.title: Shared routing rules
items.text: Leads and opportunities move through agreed ownership logic.

items.title: Shared reporting views
items.text: Both teams read the same funnel and pipeline reality.

items.title: Shared review cadence
items.text: Feedback loops are scheduled into the system.

sections.heading: Workflow integration
sections.body: The workflow should carry context from demand creation through handoff, acceptance and pipeline movement.
items: Hand off with the right context
Feed the learning back into targeting and messaging

sections.heading: Feedback loops
sections.body: Feedback loops keep the shared model honest once live work starts.
items: Accepted vs rejected leads by rule
Speed and SLA visibility across teams
Weekly review actions tied to shared data

sections.heading: Outcomes
sections.body: When alignment holds, planning gets easier because teams are finally reading the same system.
items: A tighter connection between demand and sales

en.final_cta: Align marketing and sales around one model

en.title: CRM Service | Make the core system usable
en.description: Rotata structures CRM logic so data, stages, ownership and reporting reflect the real commercial workflow.
en.h1: CRM should hold the operating model, not just the records
en.intro: The CRM is useful when it shows how work moves, who owns it and what should happen next.

sections.heading: Role in the system
sections.body: CRM is the commercial core. It stores the definitions, ownership rules and movement history that the rest of the system depends on.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.body: If this layer is weak, the wider system becomes harder to trust.
items: Data quality that does not collapse under growth

sections.heading: What breaks without it
sections.body: Weak structure leaks value in several directions at the same time.
items: Deals that progress without real qualification

sections.heading: What Rotata does
sections.body: The work is treated as system design rather than tool setup.

items.title: Data model
items.text: Define objects, properties and relationships around live decisions.

items.title: Lifecycle and stages
items.text: Reflect how demand and pipeline really behave.

items.title: Workflow rules
items.text: Make routing, tasking and owner changes visible.

items.title: Dashboards and views
items.text: Read directly from the structure the team uses every day.

sections.heading: System integration
sections.body: The layer only works when it is connected to the full operating sequence.
items: Train the team and review live usage

sections.heading: Outputs
sections.body: The output should be visible in live commercial use.
items: Reporting that stands up in weekly review

items.text: Keep unusual cases visible.

en.title: System Automation | Reduce friction without hiding logic
en.description: Rotata automates routing, tasking and owner changes only after the operating rule is explicit.
en.h1: Automation should protect speed, signal and control
en.intro: Automation is useful when it reduces manual friction without making the system harder to understand.

sections.heading: Automation role
sections.body: Automation exists to apply agreed rules consistently, on time and at scale without hiding the underlying logic.

sections.heading: Processes automated
sections.body: Automation should be used where speed, consistency and traceability matter most.

sections.heading: Logic layers
sections.body: Good automation separates triggers, conditions, ownership and exceptions so the rule can be audited later.

items.title: Trigger conditions
items.text: Define what should wake up the automation.

items.title: Qualification rules
items.text: Decide when the action is valid.

items.title: Ownership rules
items.text: Choose who receives the work next.

items.title: Exception handling
items.text: Control what happens when the normal rule should not fire.

sections.heading: Efficiency gains
sections.body: The biggest gains come from removing delay, admin work and missed next steps from the operating flow.

sections.heading: Risk handling
sections.body: Automation needs review gates, fallbacks and rollback paths so speed does not create hidden damage.
items: Use fallback owners or queues when data is incomplete
Log the action and keep rollback options visible

sections.heading: Outputs
sections.body: A strong automation layer increases speed without reducing control or auditability.

en.title: Robots | Crawl and indexing guidance for rotata.es
en.description: Understand how rotata.es publishes robots.txt, sitemap references and indexing guidance for search engines.
en.h1: Robots and crawl guidance
en.intro: This page explains how the public robots rules support a static-first, crawlable site without hiding the published content.

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.body: rotata.es publishes a simple public crawl policy intended to help search engines discover the site efficiently.

sections.heading: Purpose of robots.txt
sections.body: robots.txt gives search engines a public set of crawling instructions and points them to the sitemap used for discovery.

sections.heading: Allowed paths
sections.body: The public build is intended to be crawlable by default.
items: Public styles, scripts and image assets required for rendering
Blog article routes and legal pages
The XML sitemap published at the domain root

sections.heading: Disallowed paths
sections.body: The current public robots policy does not publish route blocks by default.
items: No public route blocks in the standard build
Unpublished source files are not exposed
404 pages are excluded from the sitemap
Future exclusions should be declared in source and deployed through the build

sections.heading: Crawl delay
sections.body: rotata.es does not publish a crawl-delay directive in its default robots output.

sections.heading: Sitemap reference
sections.body: Search engines receive a direct reference to the XML sitemap.
items: The sitemap is regenerated during the build
The sitemap is published at the domain root
The sitemap lists public HTML routes intended for discovery

sections.heading: Search engine notes
sections.body: Search engines are expected to crawl the published HTML, respect redirects and index the public site structure unless future source rules state otherwise.

sections.heading: Indexing policy
sections.body: The site is published as crawlable static output.
items: Core pages are intended for indexing
Assets needed to render the pages are available
Redirects are shipped through build artefacts when routes change

sections.heading: Updates / changes
sections.body: Any change to crawl rules, sitemap output or indexing policy should be made in source control and deployed through the build so Cloudflare and WordPress exports remain aligned.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body: Questions about crawling or indexing can be sent through the published contact routes.

en.final_cta: Use contact for an indexing question

en.title: Services | Connected capabilities inside one system
en.description: Rotata delivers CRM, data, outbound, signals and sales as connected capabilities inside one operating model.
en.h1: Services are designed to reinforce one system
en.intro: Each service exists to strengthen the wider operating model. Rotata does not treat them as standalone products.

sections.heading: Role in the system
sections.body: The service layer should make the revenue system more usable, not more fragmented.

sections.heading: Capability map
sections.body: Rotata organizes delivery around a small set of connected capabilities.

items.title: CRM
items.text: Structure stages, ownership, workflow logic and reporting around one model.

items.title: Data
items.text: Improve segmentation, enrichment and account prioritization.

items.title: Outbound
items.text: Design targeting, messaging and follow-up as a coherent motion.

items.title: Signals
items.text: Turn trigger events into timely commercial action.

items.title: Sales
items.text: Keep qualification, handoff and pipeline movement controlled.

sections.heading: Capability sequencing
sections.body: The order follows the operating break. The wrong sequence just automates the wrong constraint.

sections.heading: How the capabilities connect
sections.body: Each capability changes a different layer, but the value appears when the layers reinforce each other.
items: Data improves who enters the motion

sections.heading: Delivery model
sections.body: The engagement moves from diagnosis to build to review without breaking the model apart.

sections.heading: Outputs
sections.body: A service is only useful if its effect shows up elsewhere in the model.
